Empires & Puzzles Ana-Belle Review: The Ultimate Reset Button

Ana-Belle isn't your average healer. She doesn't just fix your team; she wipes the entire board clean of buffs and ailments. Discover why this 'Nuclear Option' remains a strategic masterpiece (and a potential headache) years after her release.
Ana-Belle Review: The Maid Who Cleanses... Literally Everything
Ana-Belle: The Maid Who Scrubs the Board Clean (Maybe Too Clean?)

Ana-Belle, the "Charlady of Morlovia," arrived with the Return to Morlovia event in 2022, bringing a mechanic that was previously reserved for the likes of Snow White: a total board reset. As a 4-star Dark (Purple) healer, she occupies a unique niche. Unlike standard healers who simply patch you up, Ana-Belle demands a clean slate, removing

. She’s the strict housekeeper who throws out your buffs along with the enemy's ailments because "clutter is clutter." While her stats have aged, her utility remains timeless for those brave enough to use her.

Ana-Belle's design is a delightful blend of "haunted mansion maid" and "undead fashionista." She sports a classic maid outfit with a Morlovia twist—blue skin, braided red hair, and a feather duster that looks like it could double as a weapon (and it does). The color palette is a vibrant mix of deep purples and crisp whites, standing out against the gloomy Halloween background. It’s a fun, character-rich design that perfectly communicates her role: she's here to clean up your mess, whether you want her to or not.

Released in 2022, Ana-Belle's base stats (683 Power) are undeniably outdated compared to the modern 4-star behemoths pushing 800 Power (like the Construct or Winter heroes of late 2025). Her defense of 697 and health of 1317 are serviceable but fragile in a world of limit-broken monsters.
As a healer, her stats are balanced enough to survive with emblems and Limit Breaks.
Without those investments (LB1/LB2), she risks vanishing before she can cast her spell. In today's meta, she is strictly a utility piece, not a tank.
"Tidy Up!" is a double-edged sword wrapped in a feather duster.

(allies and enemies) is her defining feature. This instantly neutralizes heavy buffers (like Ludwig or intricate stackers) and heavy ailment dealers (like the 5-star DoT monsters). However, it also wipes your own attack buffs, taunts, and counterattacks.

A base 45% heal is massive for Average speed. The scaling mechanic—adding 2% per removed effect up to 65%—means that in a "dirty" fight full of buffs and ailments, she can heal your team to full health in one go.

the game state when things go wrong. She is the "Abort Mission" button that stabilizes the board.
Let's look at the math behind the maid:
Average. With the Morlovia family bonus (up to +12% mana gen) and a level 23 mana troop or bards, she can functionally charge much faster, often keeping pace with Fast enemies.
45% of max HP. For a hero with 1500 HP, that's ~675 healing.
65% of max HP. If she clears 10 effects (very easy in modern wars), she heals for nearly 1000 HP per hero. That is 5-star level healing on a 4-star chassis.
Infinite. Whether the enemy has 1 buff or 50 stacks, they are all gone. The value here is binary: either she saves you from a wipe, or she does nothing.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Morlovia family bonus provides a Mana Generation boost. This is excellent for a healer, allowing her to break mana breakpoints easier. Even a solo Ana-Belle benefits, but pairing her with another (like Matilda or Alucard) ramps this up significantly.
This passive is fantastic. When she casts, she has a 65% chance to steal the newest buff
she wipes the board (or after, depending on the mechanic timing, but usually passives trigger on cast).
If she steals a buff, she gains it. Then her skill "Tidy Up!" fires and dispels
. Yes, she often cleanses her own stolen buff! The primary value here is often the fallback: if no enemy has buffs to steal, she deals
. Given her role is often to counter buff-heavy teams, she might steal (and lose) a buff, but if you fire her first to open a fight, she adds a nice chunk of chip damage.
A standard, boring, but practical defensive boost. For a healer who needs to survive to reset the board, this is exactly what she needs.
Upon release, Ana-Belle was hailed as the "Rigard Killer" by some, though veterans quickly realized she was a different beast entirely. She was the answer to the heavy buff meta of 2022.
Her stats have fallen behind significantly. She is no longer sturdy enough for top-tier 5-star wars without maximum investment (LB2). However, her skill is mechanically unique.

You don't put her on defense anymore. But on offense? She is still the queen of turning a losing battle into a neutral one. In 4-star tournaments (especially Rush or Bloody Battle where buffs/ailments are key), she is a tactical nuke.
, which allows her to resist mana ailments—a huge plus for a healer. The Costume (Sorcerer) offers higher stats and a higher healing ceiling (up to 73%).

As a healer with outdated stats, survival is paramount.

Removes taunts, dodges, damage shares, and nasty DoTs simultaneously.

The fallback 150% damage is a nice bonus for a passive healer.

She counters the heavy buffers/ailment casters prevalent here.

Ana-Belle requires a team that doesn't rely on long-term buffs.
Pair her with heroes who deal instant damage or have undispellable stacks.
Ana-Belle + Stackers (e.g., Slayer family or new Construct heroes) + Instant Snipers. Stacks cannot be dispelled, so Tidy Up won't hurt your Rayne or heavy hitters' stacks.
Since she dispels buffs but not minions, minion summoners are great partners.
Ana-Belle + Freya/Bera + Direct Damage. The minions provide the "buff" (meat shield) that she won't strip.
Do NOT pair her with heavy buffers like Black Knight, Sif, or Wilbur. She will erase their hard work.
If you pull her, level her! A healer who cleanses is vital. Just remember: fire her special
before your other heroes buff up. She is a great "oops" button when you accidentally let the enemy cast too many ailments on you.

She is a luxury tool. Worth keeping for War depth, especially against specific 5-star defenses that rely on "unbreakable" setups like Taunt + Dodge. However, do not prioritize her over modern 5-star healers unless you specifically lack a dispeller. The Costume version updates her stats nicely, making her relevant again.
"Rigard is finally outdated... The best 4-star healer?"
- This was the sentiment in 2022. While Rigard (especially C2) remains a staple due to his attack buff, Ana-Belle found her own lane.
"She’s the strict librarian of E&P. 'Quiet please, no buffs allowed!'"
"I finally retired good old Rigard... Emblems are on Ana Belle now for good."
- A bold move from the forum archives!
"The future of the game is the overheal. Ana Belle is great but for me no 4-star healer is better than Gullinbursti."
- A prescient comment; boosted health did indeed become king, which is one of Ana-Belle's few drawbacks (no overheal).

Ana-Belle is the definition of a "technical" hero. She isn't a hammer; she's a wrench. Sometimes you don't need to hit harder; you just need to take the enemy's toys away. In that role, she is unmatched in the 4-star arena. While her base stats are dusty, her kit is pristine. If you have the costume, she is a serious contender. If not, the base version is still a worthy backup for when the magic meta gets too chaotic.
A timeless utility hero who has aged gracefully in skill, if not in stats. Keep her for the rainy days when you need to wipe the slate clean.
A comprehensive review of Ana-Belle, the 4-star Halloween hero who dispels EVERYTHING. Is her 'Tidy Up' skill a lifesaver or a liability in the current meta?
